headline: Echoblast Raises $7M Seed Round Led by Conviction Partners
rendered_text:
**FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE**

**Echoblast Raises $7M Seed Round Led by Conviction Partners to Accelerate GPU Infrastructure and AI Training Platform**

SAN FRANCISCO — October 10, 2025 — Echoblast, Inc., an AI neocloud platform combining GPU rental with managed training and inference services, today announced it has raised $7 million in seed funding led by Conviction Partners. The round will fuel expansion of Echoblast's distributed GPU fleet and accelerate development of its end-to-end model training and deployment platform.

"This funding validates our thesis that AI teams need a simpler, more cost-effective alternative to building and managing their own infrastructure," said Will Coleman, CEO and co-founder of Echoblast. "Conviction Partners understood immediately why researchers and engineers are frustrated with existing cloud providers. We're going to use this capital to make GPU access frictionless."

Echoblast launched in January 2025 as part of Y Combinator's Winter 2025 batch. The platform addresses a critical pain point: most AI development teams spend weeks provisioning compute, configuring networking, and managing cluster operations—work that distracts from model development. Echoblast abstracts away that complexity, offering pre-optimized GPU instances alongside managed training pipelines, distributed inference APIs, and integrated monitoring.

The seed round will be deployed across three strategic priorities. First, Echoblast will expand its GPU fleet across North American data centers, prioritizing high-bandwidth interconnects required for large-scale distributed training. Second, the company will accelerate product development on its managed training service, adding support for multi-node orchestration, automated hyperparameter tuning, and real-time cost optimization. Third, Echoblast will grow its go-to-market team to serve early-stage AI labs, research institutions, and enterprise ML teams.

"Echoblast's founding team—Will, Mark Howell, Angela Holmes, and Mark Leonard—brings deep expertise in distributed systems, GPU scheduling, and cloud infrastructure," said a partner at Conviction Partners. "They're building the infrastructure layer that AI development will run on. We're excited to back them."

Early traction has been strong. Since launch, Echoblast has onboarded over 50 research teams and small AI companies, collectively running more than 2,000 GPU hours per week on the platform. Customers report 40-60% cost savings versus major cloud providers, combined with 10x faster provisioning times.

Echoblast's competitive advantage rests on three pillars: purpose-built infrastructure optimized for AI workloads, transparent per-minute pricing with no lock-in contracts, and a product team that listens obsessively to customer feedback. The platform supports NVIDIA H100, H200, and L40S GPUs, with support for AMD MI300 series and custom silicon coming in Q1 2026.

"We're not trying to be AWS," Coleman added. "We're building the best home for AI compute. That focus is everything."

**About Echoblast**

Echoblast, Inc. is an AI neocloud platform that combines GPU rental with managed training and inference services. Founded in January 2025 and based in San Francisco, Echoblast serves research teams, startups, and enterprises building large language models, computer vision systems, and other AI applications. The company is backed by Conviction Partners and Y Combinator. Learn more at echoblast.ai.

**Forward-Looking Statements**

This press release contains forward-looking statements regarding Echoblast's product roadmap, market opportunity, and financial projections. Actual results may differ materially. See echoblast.ai/legal for full disclaimers.
